services.name: pub.smime:createCertsOnlyData
services.description: WmPublic. Generates a PKCS #7 certs-only S/MIME entity from an array of certificates.

input_parameters.name: certificates
input_parameters.description: byte[ ][ ] The certificates that are to be encapsulated within the S/MIME entity. Each byte[ ] represents a single certificate.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream S/MIME entity.

usage_notes: For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:createEncryptedData

input_parameters.name: envStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream MIME message that you want to encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: recipientCerts
input_parameters.description: byte[ ][ ] The X.509 certificates of the recipients for whom this message will be encrypted. Each element in the list represents a certificate for a single recipient in the form of a byte[ ]. Note: When you have multiple recipients, createEncryptedData creates a single message that is encrypted for all recipients. It does not create a separate message for each recipient.

input_parameters.name: encryptionAlg
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES, or RC2. Note: If you have enabled the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode , then Integration Server uses onlyTripleDES.

input_parameters.name: keyLength
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Length of the encryption key for RC2 encryption. Must be 40, 64, or 128 (default). This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The encrypted MIME message.

usage_notes: For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:createSignedAndEncryptedData
services.description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Replaced by pub.smime.keystore:createSignedAndEncryptedData. Digitally signs a MIME message and then encrypts it.

input_parameters.name: envStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The MIME message that you want to sign and encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: privKey
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Private key of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: certificates
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] [ ] Optional. The certificate chain of the party signing the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following list shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

input_parameters.name: signerCert
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Digital certificate of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: explicit
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is to be generated. Set to: true to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. false to generate an implicit signature.

input_parameters.name: recipientCerts
input_parameters.description: byte[ ][ ] X.509 certificates of the recipients for whom this message will be encrypted. Each element in the list contains the certificate for a single recipient in the form of a byte array.

input_parameters.name: encryptionAlg
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES, or RC2. Note: If you have enabled the FIPS (Federal Information Processing Standards) mode , then Integration Server uses onlyTripleDES.

input_parameters.name: keyLength
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Length of the encryption key for RC2 encryption. Must be 40, 64, or 128 (default). This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream Signed and encrypted MIME message.

usage_notes: For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:createSignedData
services.description: WmPublic. Deprecated - Replaced by pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData. Digitally signs a MIME message.

input_parameters.name: envStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream MIME message that you want to sign (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: privKey
input_parameters.description: byte[] Private key of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: certificates
input_parameters.description: byte[ ][ ] Optional. Certificate chain of the party that signed the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

input_parameters.name: signerCert
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Digital certificate of the party signing the message.

input_parameters.name: explicit
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is generated. Set to: true to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. false to generate an implicit signature.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The signed MIME message.

usage_notes: This service is superseded by pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData.
For general information about MIME messages and using the MIME services, see the MIME-S/MIME Developer’s Guide .

services.name: pub.smime:processCertsOnlyData
services.description: WmPublic. Extracts the certificates from a PKCS #7 certs-only S/MIME entity.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The certs-only S/MIME entity.

output_parameters.name: certificates
output_parameters.description: byte[ ][ ] The extracted certificates. Each element in the list contains one of the extracted certificates represented as a byte[ ].

services.name: pub.smime:processEncryptedData

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The encrypted S/MIME entity (for example, the output produced by pub.smime:createEncryptedData).

input_parameters.name: recipientCert
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Digital certificate of the party receiving the message.

input_parameters.name: privKey
input_parameters.description: byte[ ] Private key of the party receiving the message (that is, the party whose public key was used to encrypt the message).

output_parameters.name: mimeData
output_parameters.description: Document MIME object containing the decrypted MIME message.

output_parameters.name: contentDigest
output_parameters.description: String Message digest of the encrypted content, base64-encoded. (Some sites return this digest to the sender to acknowledge their receipt of the message.)

output_parameters.name: encrypted
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Flag indicating whether the decrypted MIME entity is encrypted. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is encrypted. false indicates that the MIME entity is not encrypted.

output_parameters.name: signed
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Flag indicating whether the decrypted MIME entity is signed. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is signed. false indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

output_parameters.name: certsOnly
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Flag indicating whether the decrypted MIME entity is a certs-only entity. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity. false indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

output_parameters.name: stream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream Conditional. The decrypted MIME entity.

usage_notes: This service is superseded by pub.smime.keystore:processEncryptedData.

If the decrypted message is signed or encrypted, mimeData will be empty, and the decrypted message will reside in stream. You can check the state of the signed and encrypted output variables to determine whether the decrypted message requires additional processing, and pass stream to the pub.smime:processSignedData or pub.smime:processEncryptedData service as necessary.

services.name: pub.smime:processSignedData
services.description: WmPublic. Verifies the signature from a signed S/MIME entity and extracts the message from it.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream Signed MIME entity (for example, the output produced by pub.smime:createSignedData).

input_parameters.name: signerCertChain
input_parameters.description: byte[ ][ ] Optional. Certificate chain of the party that signed the message, where each byte[ ] represents a single certificate in the chain. Certificates must appear in hierarchical order, starting with the signer's certificate in element 0. The following shows how the elements of a complete chain would appear for a certificate that was issued through two intermediate CAs:

input_parameters.name: signerCertChain
input_parameters.description: Note: If the signer included the certificate chain with the digital signature, you do not need to supply signerCertChain.

output_parameters.name: mimeData
output_parameters.description: Document MIME object containing the extracted MIME entity.

output_parameters.name: contentDigest
output_parameters.description: String Message digest (base64-encoded) that was recalculated by processSignedData.

output_parameters.name: signerCert
output_parameters.description: java.security.cert.X509Certificate Signer's X.509 certificate.

output_parameters.name: encrypted
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Flag indicating whether the extracted MIME entity is encrypted. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is encrypted. false indicates that the MIME entity is not encrypted.

output_parameters.name: signed
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Flag indicating whether the extracted MIME entity is signed. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is signed. false indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

output_parameters.name: certsOnly
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Flag indicating whether the extracted MIME entity is a certs-only entity. A value of: true indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity. false indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

output_parameters.name: stream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream Conditional. Extracted MIME entity.

output_parameters.name: verify
output_parameters.description: String Flag indicating whether the signature was successfully processed. Success indicates that the signature was successfully verified with the supplied public key. A value of: true indicates that signature processing was successful. false indicates that signature processing failed. The signature could not be verified because an errorCode 1, 2, 3, or 4 occurred.

output_parameters.name: trusted
output_parameters.description: String Flag indicating whether the signer certificate is trusted or not. A value of: true indicates that the signer certificate is trusted. false indicates that the signer certificate is not trusted.

output_parameters.name: errorCode
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Number indicating the kind of error that occurred while processing the signature. See errorMessage for possible values. If no error occurred, errorCode will not be returned.

output_parameters.name: errorMessage
output_parameters.description: String Conditional. Textual error message indicating what kind of error occurred while processing the signature. Error codes and messages are as follows:

output_parameters.name: errorCode
output_parameters.description: errorMessage

usage_notes: If verify is false, the errorCode and errorMessage values will indicate the error that caused the failure. Note that errorCode values 5 through 7 do not represent signature-validation failures and, therefore, do not cause the verify flag to be set to false.

If the extracted entity is signed or encrypted, mimeData will be empty, and the extracted entity will reside in stream. You can check the state of the signed and encrypted output variables to determine whether the extracted entity requires additional processing, and pass stream to the pub.smime:processEncryptedData service as necessary.

services.name: pub.smime.keystore:createSignedAndEncryptedData
services.description: WmPublic. Digitally signs and encrypts a MIME message.

input_parameters.name: envStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The MIME message that you want to sign and encrypt (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: keyStoreAlias
input_parameters.description: String Alias of the keystore containing the signing key.

input_parameters.name: keyAlias
input_parameters.description: String Alias of the private key to be used for signing.

input_parameters.name: explicit
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is to be generated. Set to: True to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. False to generate an implicit signature.

input_parameters.name: recipientCerts
input_parameters.description: Object List A list of byte[ ] for the partner certificates for whom this message will be encrypted.

input_parameters.name: encryptionAlg
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Code specifying the encryption algorithm to use. Must be TripleDES (default), DES, or RC2.

input_parameters.name: keyLength
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Length of the encryption key for RC2 encryption. Must be 40, 64, or 128 (default). This parameter is ignored if encryptionAlg is not RC2.

input_parameters.name: signingAlg
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The signing algorithm to use to sign the S/MIME messages. Set to: SHA224 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 The default is SHA256.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream Signed and encrypted data as a stream.

usage_notes: You must use this service when you want to create a message that is both signed and encrypted.
For information about using aliases for keystores, truststores, and private keys, see Creating a Keystore and Truststore.

services.name: pub.smime.keystore:createSignedData

input_parameters.name: envStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream MIME message that you want to sign (for example, the output produced by pub.mime:getEnvelopeStream).

input_parameters.name: keyStoreAlias
input_parameters.description: String Alias of the keystore.

input_parameters.name: keyAlias
input_parameters.description: String Alias of the private key of interest in the keystore.

input_parameters.name: explicit
input_parameters.description: String Optional. Flag indicating whether an implicit or explicit signature is generated. Set to: True to generate an explicit (detached) signature. This is the default. False to generate an implicit signature.

input_parameters.name: signingAlg
input_parameters.description: String Optional. The signing algorithm to use to sign the S/MIME messages. Set to: SHA224 SHA256 SHA384 SHA512 The default is SHA256.

output_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The signed MIME stream.

usage_notes: For information about using aliases for keystores, truststores, and private keys, see Creating a Keystore and Truststore.

services.name: pub.smime.keystore:processEncryptedData
services.description: WmPublic. Decrypts an encrypted S/MIME message.

input_parameters.name: SMimeEnvStream
input_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The encrypted S/MIME stream.

input_parameters.name: keyStoreAlias
input_parameters.description: String Alias of the keystore containing the decryption key.

input_parameters.name: keyAlias
input_parameters.description: String Alias of the key used for decryption.

output_parameters.name: mimeData
output_parameters.description: Document The decrypted MIME message.

output_parameters.name: contentDigest
output_parameters.description: String Message digest of the encrypted content, base64-encoded. (Some sites return this digest to the sender to acknowledge their receipt of the message.

output_parameters.name: encrypted
output_parameters.description: String Flag indicating whether the MIME entity passed in to the service was encrypted. A value of: True indicates that the MIME entity was encrypted. False indicates that the MIME entity was not encrypted.

output_parameters.name: signed
output_parameters.description: String Flag indicating whether the MIME entity passed in to the service was signed. A value of: True indicates that the MIME entity is signed. False indicates that the MIME entity is not signed.

output_parameters.name: certsOnly
output_parameters.description: String Flag indicating whether the MIME entity passed in to the service contained only digital certificates. A value of: True indicates that the MIME entity is a certs-only entity. False indicates that the MIME entity is not a certs-only entity.

output_parameters.name: stream
output_parameters.description: java.io.InputStream The decrypted MIME entity.

usage_notes: For information about using aliases for keystores, truststores, and private keys, see Creating a Keystore and Truststore.
